expectations.evidence: Step 5: 'User says "execute ... plan" and provides a plan file — this is Mode 4: Plan Execution. The skill reads docs/plan.md to extract tasks.' Mode 4 explicitly detected.

expectations.text: Classifies tasks into code (registration, login) and non-code (migration, config, docs) types
expectations.evidence: Step 8 Task Classification table: Task 1 (DB migration) = non-code (task), Task 2 (User CRUD) = code, Task 3 (Env config) = non-code (task), Task 4 (API endpoints) = code, Task 5 (API docs) = non-code (task). Classification with reasoning provided for each.

expectations.text: Routes code tasks through the full TDD pipeline (Test Writer -> RED -> Code Writer -> GREEN -> Review)
expectations.evidence: Unit user-crud: Steps 4a (Test Writer) -> 4b (RED) -> 4c (Code Writer) -> 4d (GREEN) -> 4e/4f/4g (reviews). Unit user-api: same full pipeline. Both code tasks go through the complete TDD cycle.

expectations.text: Routes non-code tasks through implementer dispatch (Implementer -> Spec Compliance -> Code Quality)
expectations.evidence: Unit database-schema: Step 4h (Implementer Dispatch) -> 4e (Spec Compliance) -> 4g (Code Quality). Unit env-config: same pattern. Unit api-docs: same pattern. All three non-code tasks use implementer dispatch with spec compliance and code quality reviews. Adversarial review skipped for non-code tasks.

expectations.text: Respects dependency ordering — migration before registration, registration before login, config before login, both before docs
expectations.evidence: Execution timeline: t0 dispatches database-schema and env-config (no deps). t1: database-schema completes, user-crud starts. t3: user-crud completes + env-config already done, user-api starts. t4: user-api completes, api-docs starts. DAG shown: 'database-schema -> user-crud -> user-api -> api-docs' and 'env-config -> user-api'. All dependencies respected.

expectations.text: Presents a work plan with eager dispatch format showing dependency chains, not rigid batches
expectations.evidence: User Confirmation shows: 'Execution plan (eager dispatch, max 4 concurrent): Ready immediately: database-schema, env-config (parallel). After database-schema: user-crud. After user-crud + env-config: user-api. After user-api: api-docs.' This shows dependency chains, not batch levels.

expectations.text: Uses eager dispatch — units start as soon as their specific dependencies complete, not when an entire level finishes
expectations.evidence: Step 10 KEY EAGER DISPATCH BEHAVIOR: 'user-crud starts as soon as database-schema completes. It does NOT wait for env-config to finish, even though they were dispatched in the same initial wave. This is the core advantage of eager over batch dispatch.' Timeline confirms user-crud starts at t1 (database-schema done), not t2 (env-config done).

expectations.text: Applies the subagent status protocol (DONE/DONE_WITH_CONCERNS/NEEDS_CONTEXT/BLOCKED) to all agents
expectations.evidence: All agents report status: Test Writers report 'DONE', Code Writers report 'DONE', Implementers report 'DONE'. Summary mentions: 'The subagent status protocol is used for all agents' and 'the implementer prompt explicitly encourages subagents to report NEEDS_CONTEXT or BLOCKED rather than guessing.' The protocol is applied across both code and non-code agent types.

expectations.text: Runs a final holistic review across all tasks after individual completion
expectations.evidence: Step 11 Phase 5: 'Run full test suite: npx vitest run. All code tests pass. Verify non-code artifacts: migration file exists, config file exists, OpenAPI spec exists. Cross-unit integration: user-api correctly calls user-crud, uses env-config values. End-to-end: start server, hit endpoints (if possible).'

claims: Mode 4 (plan execution) correctly detected and handles mixed code/non-code task plans
Task classification separates code tasks (TDD pipeline) from non-code tasks (implementer dispatch)
Dependency DAG correctly built from 5-task plan with diamond-shaped dependencies
Eager dispatch demonstrated: user-crud starts when database-schema finishes, not when env-config finishes
Subagent status protocol applied uniformly across Test Writers, Code Writers, and Implementers
Final holistic review covers both code artifacts (test suite) and non-code artifacts (migration, config, docs)

suggestions: Consider adding an assertion about inline task provision: the trace mentions subagents receive task text inline (not file references), which is a key skill feature for plan execution.
The trace does not exercise the NEEDS_CONTEXT or BLOCKED status paths. A variant where a task fails or has unresolvable dependencies would test the escalation protocol.
The execution timeline visualization is excellent for understanding eager dispatch. Consider whether the eval should explicitly verify the timeline matches expectations.
eval_feedback.overall: The with_skill run demonstrates the most complex orchestration scenario in the eval suite. All 9 assertions pass. Plan execution with mixed code/non-code tasks, complex dependency chains, eager dispatch, and the subagent status protocol are all correctly exercised. The eager dispatch advantage is clearly demonstrated: user-crud starts immediately when its dependency completes, without waiting for unrelated tasks.
